nN.description: Total number of nodes in the supply network (suppliers, transshipment points, and beneficiary camps)

nP.description: Number of simple paths from a supplier to a beneficiary camp

nC.description: Number of cycles in the supply network

S.description: Supplier node indices: S[s] is the index in N of supplier s

T.description: Transshipment node indices: T[t] is the index in N of transshipment node t

B.description: Beneficiary camp node indices: B[j] is the index in N of beneficiary camp j

E.description: Adjacency matrix: 1 if a directed edge exists from node i to node j, 0 otherwise

pE.description: Path-edge indicator: 1 if edge (i, j) is part of path p, 0 otherwise

pRank.description: Rank/position of node v within path p; strictly increases along path edges

pCost.description: Shipping cost per kg of commodity k along path p

cE.description: Cycle-edge indicator: 1 if edge (i, j) is part of cycle c, 0 otherwise

cCost.description: Shipping cost per kg of commodity k along cycle c

q.description: Procurement cost per kg of commodity k

nutval.description: Nutritional value per kg of commodity k for nutrient l

nutreq.description: Per-person nutritional requirement for nutrient l

dem.description: Number of beneficiaries at beneficiary camp j

e.description: Indicator: 1 if path p ends at beneficiary camp j, 0 otherwise

assumptions.description: There is at least one node.

code.python: assert nN >= 1

assumptions.description: There is at least one supplier node.

code.python: assert nS >= 1

assumptions.description: There is at least one transshipment node.

code.python: assert nT >= 1

assumptions.description: There is at least one beneficiary camp.

code.python: assert nB >= 1

assumptions.description: There is at least one commodity.

code.python: assert nK >= 1

assumptions.description: There is at least one nutrient.

code.python: assert nL >= 1

assumptions.description: Each supplier node index is a valid node index (in range [0, nN)).

code.python: assert all(0 <= S[s] < nN for s in range(nS))

assumptions.description: Each transshipment node index is a valid node index (in range [0, nN)).

code.python: assert all(0 <= T[t] < nN for t in range(nT))

assumptions.description: Each beneficiary camp node index is a valid node index (in range [0, nN)).

code.python: assert all(0 <= B[j] < nN for j in range(nB))

assumptions.description: The supplier, transshipment, and beneficiary node-class maps partition N (every node belongs to exactly one class).
assumptions.formulation: N = \{S_s : s \in N_S\} \sqcup \{T_t : t \in N_T\} \sqcup \{B_j : j \in N_B\}

code.python: assert set(S) | set(T) | set(B) == set(range(nN)) and set(S).isdisjoint(T) and set(S).isdisjoint(B) and set(T).isdisjoint(B)

assumptions.description: The supplier, transshipment, and beneficiary node-class maps are each injective.

code.python: assert len(set(S)) == nS and len(set(T)) == nT and len(set(B)) == nB

assumptions.description: Each path edge must be a graph edge.
assumptions.formulation: pE_{p,i,j} \leq E_{ij} \quad \forall p \in P, i, j \in N

code.python: assert all(pE[p][i][j] <= E[i][j] for p in range(nP) for i in range(nN) for j in range(nN))

assumptions.description: Within each path, every node has in-degree at most 1.
assumptions.formulation: \sum_{i \in N} pE_{p,i,v} \leq 1 \quad \forall p \in P, v \in N

code.python: assert all(sum(pE[p][i][v] for i in range(nN)) <= 1 for p in range(nP) for v in range(nN))

assumptions.description: Within each path, every node has out-degree at most 1.
assumptions.formulation: \sum_{j \in N} pE_{p,v,j} \leq 1 \quad \forall p \in P, v \in N

code.python: assert all(sum(pE[p][v][j] for j in range(nN)) <= 1 for p in range(nP) for v in range(nN))

assumptions.description: Each path has a unique source node (in-degree 0, out-degree 1) and that node is a supplier.
assumptions.formulation: \forall p \in P, \exists! v \in N : \sum_{j \in N} pE_{p,v,j} = 1 \land \sum_{i \in N} pE_{p,i,v} = 0; \text{ moreover } v \in \{S_s : s \in N_S\}

assumptions.description: Each path has a unique sink node (in-degree 1, out-degree 0) and that node is a beneficiary camp.
assumptions.formulation: \forall p \in P, \exists! v \in N : \sum_{i \in N} pE_{p,i,v} = 1 \land \sum_{j \in N} pE_{p,v,j} = 0; \text{ moreover } v \in \{B_b : b \in N_B\}

assumptions.description: End indicator e is consistent with pE: e_{j,p} = 1 iff path p ends at beneficiary camp j.
assumptions.formulation: e_{j,p} = \sum_{i \in N} pE_{p,i,B_j} - \sum_{k \in N} pE_{p,B_j,k} \quad \forall j \in N_B, p \in P

code.python: assert all(e[j][p] == sum(pE[p][i][B[j]] for i in range(nN)) - sum(pE[p][B[j]][k] for k in range(nN)) for j in range(nB) for p in range(nP))

assumptions.description: Each path is acyclic: rank strictly increases along path edges.
assumptions.formulation: pE_{p,i,j} = 1 \implies pRank_{p,j} = pRank_{p,i} + 1 \quad \forall p \in P, i, j \in N

code.python: assert all(pRank[p][j] == pRank[p][i] + 1 for p in range(nP) for i in range(nN) for j in range(nN) if pE[p][i][j] == 1)

assumptions.description: Path indexing is complete: every valid simple supplier-to-beneficiary path is indexed by some p in P.
assumptions.formulation: \forall \text{ valid simple } S\text{-to-}B \text{ path } \pi, \exists p \in P : pE_p = \pi

code.python: # Completeness must be verified by external path enumeration; not asserted here.

assumptions.description: Path indexing is injective: distinct path indices give distinct edge sets.
assumptions.formulation: p_1 \neq p_2 \implies \exists i, j \in N : pE_{p_1,i,j} \neq pE_{p_2,i,j}

code.python: assert len({tuple(tuple(row) for row in pE[p]) for p in range(nP)}) == nP

assumptions.description: Shipping cost along each path for each commodity is non-negative.

code.python: assert all(pCost[p][k] >= 0 for p in range(nP) for k in range(nK))

assumptions.description: Each cycle edge must be a graph edge.
assumptions.formulation: cE_{c,i,j} \leq E_{ij} \quad \forall c \in C, i, j \in N

code.python: assert all(cE[c][i][j] <= E[i][j] for c in range(nC) for i in range(nN) for j in range(nN))

assumptions.description: Within each cycle, every node has in-degree at most 1.
assumptions.formulation: \sum_{i \in N} cE_{c,i,v} \leq 1 \quad \forall c \in C, v \in N

code.python: assert all(sum(cE[c][i][v] for i in range(nN)) <= 1 for c in range(nC) for v in range(nN))

assumptions.description: Within each cycle, every node has out-degree at most 1.
assumptions.formulation: \sum_{j \in N} cE_{c,v,j} \leq 1 \quad \forall c \in C, v \in N

code.python: assert all(sum(cE[c][v][j] for j in range(nN)) <= 1 for c in range(nC) for v in range(nN))

assumptions.description: Within each cycle, each node's in-degree equals its out-degree (flow conservation), so every node is either skipped or visited with exactly one incoming and one outgoing edge. Together with the degree bounds this makes the active edges a node-disjoint union of simple cycles.
assumptions.formulation: \sum_{i \in N} cE_{c,i,v} = \sum_{j \in N} cE_{c,v,j} \quad \forall c \in C, v \in N

code.python: assert all(sum(cE[c][i][v] for i in range(nN)) == sum(cE[c][v][j] for j in range(nN)) for c in range(nC) for v in range(nN))

assumptions.description: Each cycle is connected: the active edges of every cycle form a single simple directed cycle rather than a union of two or more node-disjoint cycles. Equivalently, starting from any active node and following successors returns to the start only after visiting every active node.
assumptions.formulation: \{(i, j) : cE_{c,i,j} = 1\} \text{ forms a single directed cycle} \quad \forall c \in C

assumptions.description: Cycle indexing is complete: every valid cycle is indexed by some c in C.

code.python: # Completeness must be verified by external cycle enumeration; not asserted here.

assumptions.description: Cycle indexing is injective: distinct cycle indices give distinct edge sets.
assumptions.formulation: c_1 \neq c_2 \implies \exists i, j \in N : cE_{c_1,i,j} \neq cE_{c_2,i,j}

code.python: assert len({tuple(tuple(row) for row in cE[c]) for c in range(nC)}) == nC

assumptions.description: Shipping cost along each cycle for each commodity is non-negative.

code.python: assert all(cCost[c][k] >= 0 for c in range(nC) for k in range(nK))

assumptions.description: Procurement cost per kg of each commodity is non-negative.

code.python: assert all(q[k] >= 0 for k in range(nK))

assumptions.description: Nutritional value per kg of each commodity for each nutrient is non-negative.

code.python: assert all(nutval[k][l] >= 0 for k in range(nK) for l in range(nL))

assumptions.description: Per-person nutritional requirement for each nutrient is non-negative.

code.python: assert all(nutreq[l] >= 0 for l in range(nL))

assumptions.description: Number of beneficiaries at each camp is non-negative.

code.python: assert all(dem[j] >= 0 for j in range(nB))

x.description: Amount of commodity k shipped along path p (kg)

y.description: Amount of commodity k shipped along cycle c (kg)

R.description: Ration size per person of each commodity (kg)

constraints.description: Each beneficiary camp receives at least its ration demand from paths ending there for each commodity.
constraints.formulation: \sum_{p \in P} e_{jp} x_{pk} \geq \mathrm{dem}_j R_k \quad \forall j \in N_B, k \in K

constraints.description: The ration collectively satisfies all per-person nutritional requirements.
Amount shipped along each path for each commodity is non-negative.

code.gurobipy: model.addConstrs(x[p, k] >= 0 for p in range(nP) for k in range(nK))

constraints.description: Amount shipped along each cycle for each commodity is non-negative.

code.gurobipy: model.addConstrs(y[c, k] >= 0 for c in range(nC) for k in range(nK))

constraints.description: Ration size per person of each commodity is non-negative.

objective.description: Minimize total shipping cost plus total procurement cost.
objective.formulation: \min \sum_{p \in P} \sum_{k \in K} pCost_{pk} x_{pk} + \sum_{c \in C} \sum_{k \in K} cCost_{ck} y_{ck} + \sum_{k \in K} q_k \sum_{p \in P} x_{pk}

code.gurobipy:
model.setObjective(
    gp.quicksum(pCost[p][k] * x[p, k] for p in range(nP) for k in range(nK))
    + gp.quicksum(cCost[c][k] * y[c, k] for c in range(nC) for k in range(nK))
    + gp.quicksum(q[k] * gp.quicksum(x[p, k] for p in range(nP)) for k in range(nK)),
    GRB.MINIMIZE
)

notes: The inefficient formulation is path- and cycle-based.

Proving this is a valid reformulation requires introducing many assumptions that relate the path-based parameters to the underlying supply network structure of the efficient formulation. These assumptions ensure the set of paths is well-formed and allows for a flow-decomposition argument.
